text: and then 1.18.4 monks i know not of any other single person fraught with such loss to many folk such discomfort to many folk with such loss discomfort and sorrow to devas and mankind as ma kali that infatuated men just as monks at the river mouth one sets a fish trap to the discomfort suffering distress and destruction of many fish even so makali the infatuated infatuated man was born into the world meetings to be a man trapped for the discomfort suffering distress and destruction of many beings in this uttar the buddha did not hesitate to speak badly on makali gosala putta one who had wrong view because he was leading many folk into the woeful planes and sometimes people think that the buddha was such a nice man that he cannot criticize another person it's not true the buddha is willing to benefit many people and speak out now even even uh to name somebody who was leading some people to have wrong views we were at 1.18 so today we start with 1.18.13 the buddha said monks just as even a trifling bit of dung has an evil smell so likewise do i not favor existing even for a trifling time not even for the lasting of a finger snap and that's the end of the suta the buddha says that he does not want to exist even for a trifling time and existence to him is likened to dung which has a bad smell so that's how he views existence why does the buddha say that existence is uh so um uh so obnoxious and sort of so foul like uh is because even buddhas have to suffer because there are two types of suffering one is mental suffering the other one is physical suffering the buddha said that all aryas have done away with mental suffering but they still have physical suffering and we cannot run away from physical suffering as long as we have a body because with a body we have uh aging sickening and dying and the buddha said in more than one suta and when he was old that his his body was breaking up and it gave him a lot of suffering so even buddhas have to suffer and some people are of the opinion that because buddhas and arahants don't have self that they do not suffer but if that is the case then you can think for example if the buddha had physical suffering from the body then if there was no if it did not affect him there would have been no necessity for him to enter into a deep janaza or cessation of perception and feeling um which the buddha did when the pain of the body was quite unbearable he said he had to enter into deep states of meditation so that he did not experience pain in the body and then after that he comes out of it for some time there is no pain but later the pain might come back again so there's one suta in the itty but it's 2.44 there the buddha said what because is the nirvana element with residue left here a biku is an arahant one whose asavas are destroyed the holy life fulfilled who has done what had to be done laid down the burden attained the goal destroyed the fetters of being and is completely released through final knowledge however his five sense faculties remain unimpaired by which he still experiences what is agreeable and disagreeable and he feels pleasure and pain sukha and dukkha it is the extinction of attachment head and delusion in him that is called the nibana element with residue left now what because is the nibana element with no residue left then he explains the other part so here the buddha is saying because arahanna still has the five sense faculties he feels suka and dukkha so that explains why uh the buddha says that existence uh he he doesn't want to exist uh even for a short time if he if he uh if he could and this uh you'll find is quite uh a different from some later books we see that the buddha sort of come back again and again or he's always in existence now the next suta is 1.19 1.19 there are several sutas here uh but i'll just choose one of them the buddha said even as monks in this rose apple land that is jambu deeper trifling in number are the pleasant parks the pleasant groves the pleasant grounds and lakes while more numerous are the steep precipitous places unfoldable rivers dense tickets of stakes and thorns and inaccessible mountains just so few in number are those beings that welcome when they hear it the dhamma vinaya set forth by a tata gata more numerous are they that do not that's the end of the sutra here the buddha is saying that in india the accessible places where people can use are very much less than those places which are inaccessible like uh steep places mountains uh dense uh forest and jungle unfoldable rivers etc you must remember this suta was spoken two thousand five hundred years ago so the number uh the the amount of inaccessible places was much more during that time than now and the buddha said that few people and just like the the the the amount of places that are accessible so uh small amount compared to the whole of india so in the same way few people uh out of the many one to listen to dhamma vinaya dhamma vinaya is teaches us the truth or reality about existence and it is actually painful to hear the reality of existence and sometimes people don't want to face the truth they want to face their reality they would like to believe what they want to believe what they like to believe so because of that few people want to listen to the dhamma and the disciplinary code taught by the buddha the vinaya so in other words we don't expect to have many followers of the buddha's teaching in fact most buddhas when they are enlightened do not want to teach just like our buddha buddha after he was enlightened he contemplated the minds of living beings and he found that few people are inclined to listen to the dhamma and even if they listen few uh even fewer want to practice it so he actually wanted to be a pachika buddha but later he was persuaded by maha this uh brahma sahampati to turn the wheel of the dhamma and he did surah we find this happens more more than once that sometimes the buddha is not inclined to do something um somebody appeals to him to change his mind sometimes they appear once he doesn't want to change his mind then they appeal a second time still he doesn't want to change his mind but when they appeal the third time out of compassion he will change his mind and do something that at first he was not inclined to do so this shows uh that actually pachika buddhas are able to teach but it's just that they are not inclined to teach it's not like some of the later books i say that particular buddhas are unable to teach because whatever path that they have walked through is very clear to them the path that they have walked through so it shouldn't be uh difficult at all for them to teach and they would rather teach actually by example they show a good example of being a renuncian and they practice and perhaps people if people ask them in individually of the dharma they might teach but they are not inclined to teach to the whole world and turn the dharma wheel and some people think that that is selfishness but it is not selfishness because it appears to me that every being has its own time and the best teacher is suffering because when we suffer enough and it will bring us to walk the path if we have not been in samsara we are not suffered enough we are not spiritually mature even if the buddha is around we would not be inclined to follow his teaching so that was that is why we find that during the buddha's time there were many people who did not want to follow the buddha's teachings even though the buddha was in the world now the next is 1.19.2 the buddha says just as monks in this jambudipa rose apple land that is india trifling in number are the pleasant parks the pleasant groves the pleasant grounds and lakes while more numerous other steep precipitous places unfoldable rivers dense tickets of stakes and thorns and inaccessible mountains just so few in number are those beings who diseasing as men are reborn among men more numerous are those beings who diseasing as men are reborn in hell who are reborn in the wombs of animals who are reborn in the realm of ghosts in the same way jesus in india they are traveling in number other pleasant parks pleasant groves pleasant grounds and lakes while more numerous other steep precipitators places unfoldable rivers etc etc just so few in number are those beings who deceasing as men are reborn among the devas more numerous are those beings who deceasing as men are reborn in hell in the wombs of animals in the realm of ghosts so in the same way the suta continues few in number are those beings who diseasing as devas are reborn as devas or are reborn as men more numerous are those beings who is deceasing as devas are born into the three woeful planes of hell the animal realm and the realm of ghost in the same way similarly few in number are those beings who deceasing from hell or from the animal realm or from the ghost realm are reborn as men or as devas more numerous are those beings who deceasing from hell from the animal realm from the ghost realm are reborn back into the hell realm or in the animal realm or in the ghost realm ah that's the end of the sutal so basically what this is saying is that most men after they pass away they are not they don't come back into the human realm or go up into the deva realm instead most of them go into the warfoot plains the three woeful planes hell animal realm and the ghost realm similarly devas most of the devas when they pass away are reborn into the woeful plains few of them are reborn backed as devas or as human beings and even the beings from the woeful planes after they pass away most of them are reborn back into the woefu planes and not into the human realm or the heavenly realm now what amount we born it back into the woefu planes for example human beings uh i would give a a very rough estimate perhaps out of every 10 people out of every 10 human beings maybe only two or three yeah come back into the human realm human world or are reborn as devas maybe seven or eight out of ten people go down into the whole food plains so from there we can realize that it is quite uh dangerous uh and uh we should not be like most people most ordinary people now they live a very ordinary life and then they go into the uh woeful planes because they follow their inclinations they follow all their lower instincts it would appear like the number of beings in the in the in the world every world system is like a pyramid you know the higher you go the less beings there are the lower you go the more beings there are the base is very wide the top is very narrow now the other thing here you can see here is that then the woefu planes mentioned here and throughout the sutas are only three you know that is the first one the lowest one is the health realm and slightly above that is the animal realm and and better than the animal realm according to samsuta is the realm of ghosts so there are only three planes of existence in the woefu planes later books added another plane called the asura plane but when we check with the sutas we find that it contradicts the suta because in the sutta two sutas in the diga nikaya one says that the lowest type of asuras are the kalakanja asuras and then later in another suta it is stated that the kala kanja asuras are also devas so you can see here that if the lowest kind of asuras are devas then all of the asuras must be devasta so there are only three woeful planes and not four according to the sutas now the next suta is 1.20.1 [Music] the buddha said truthfully monks these are to be reckoned as gains forest dwelling living on arms wearing rag ropes wearing three ropes only teaching the dhamma mastery of the vinaya wide knowledge the rank of an elder teera the blessing of true department the blessing of a following the blessing of a large following as a man of good family a flare a fair complexion pleasant speech to be content with little and freedom from sickness now that's the end of the sutta so here the buddha is saying these things are really worth gaining these are good things to have to practice i would give you a lot of blessing the first one is forest dwelling this is basically for mangala the word forest is actually a translation of the word i think arania aranya is basically a secluded place a quiet place and usually the buddha mentions a place like a cave up in the fall up in the hills um very quiet secluded place basically because if a monk wants to practice well it is best to to to live in such places the second one also for monks is living on arms that means to go on arms round this is a very good practice for monks because it is not something easy to do sometimes you go on arms around people are quite rude to you they just ask you to shovel or something like that and or they just ignore you or they give you a roots there or something and uh it's uh very good for our ego if we want to wear away our ego these are some of the things which are very helpful that's why it's not easy to be among some people they don't realize that and they i don't appreciate monks enough and that's a loss to them third one is wearing rag ropes rag ropes here is a translation for uh this rope this this the meaning of it now is that it is picked up a cloth that is picked up during the buddha's time according to the later books uh something like uh 20 or 25 years the first 20 or 25 years of the buddha's ministry ministry uh he and his disciples only wore uh robes that were made from uh a cloth that was picked up and they would go to these what they call channel grounds cemeteries places where dead bodies were thrown and um the cloth that was left over and the mouse would go and pick it up or from whatever place the cloth was thrown away they would pick it up and sow them into a rope but later the lay disciples of the buddha asked the buddha for permission to give ropes to the monks and out of compassion for them to give people a chance to cultivate marina the buddha allowed uh lay people to make offerings to uh monks uh sometimes uh ap some people don't uh don't realize that it is uh the good fortune to be able to give to monks and uh that is uh that is the the buddha's so this about red robes and the third one the fourth one is wearing three ropes only the ropes of a manga are generally stated to be three one is the sabung or one sometimes it's called the sarong and then this rope that we are wearing there's a shivarra shivon and then there's another one that's two-layer double lid that is for use as a blanket now then teaching dhamma teaching dhamma is also a good thing the buddha encouraged the teaching of dhamma that's why the buddha you find during the buddha's time and up to now that the buddha encouraged his monks to teach the dhamma and not all monks taught dhamma and not all monks taught meditation there were some monks who were meditation monks and some monks who were dharma preachers and some monks who mastered both and during the buddha's time there were many types of monks and the buddha allowed all of them to do what was they what they thought was best for them and the mastery of the vinaya uh another one also concerning monks is mastery of the rules and the disciplinary rules and if they could live by the disciplinary code that would accumulate they would accumulate a lot of blessings the next one is white knowledge white knowledge is a translation for bahu sacha that means a lot of knowledge of the dharma the buddha said now very few people can teach the very few people can teach the dhamma according to the the real uh essence the real dhamma that the buddha taught then the next one is the rank of an elder an elder here is a monk is one who has 10 vasas and above a man who has 10 vasas and above is a man who can ordain new monks among who has 10 vassals and above can accept disciples the buddha did not allow a younger monk to accept disciples but the buddha asked the monks to practice for the first 10 years to get a lot of experience then after that only to accept disciples that's why uh to be an elder is a blessing the blessing of true department that means a man who can conduct himself well and that is uh a good blessing then the blessing of a following that means uh if a monk can have a following of disciples that that is a blessing um this following can be lay disciples or among disciples and during the buddha's time we find that several monks they had a large following of among disciples and nowadays we find that not many monks are able to have a following of among disciples some of them have a following of lay disciples but to be had to have a following of among disciples it is very good especially for the religion because there is always a shortage of monks the blessing of a large following to have a a big following of lay disciples and among disciples during the buddha's time he sometimes he would walk around with 1250 monks and you go from place to place and that would be really impressive and the buddha why did the buddha take such a large following with him it was not that i mean it's really very difficult to look after such a large amount of monks and to travel in the group it's a lot of it's like an army moving logistics involved to look after their food and all that but uh that is is it's not easy to have a large following because unless that person has enough meta and uh enough ability to teach there would not be many monks who would want to follow him a man of good family that means to be born into a good family a good family is not necessarily a rich family good family is one which is upright who knows the dhamma who follows the the dhamma straightforward simple people it's not easy to find sometimes a flare a fair complexion fair complexion i think you'll understand pleasant speech to talk uh pleasantly this is one of the qualities that endear dear person to others if a person speaks very rudely unpleasant speech nobody likes to be near him or her the buddha said all of us are born with an axe in our tongue in the dharmapatha here if you're not careful we simply swing the x and we hurt ourselves to be content with little uh somebody who has few a few wishes a few things to ask for and freedom from sickness this is another great blessing to be seldom signal now the next two ties one uh 1.20.2 the buddha said monks if even for the lasting of a finger snap a monk should practice the first jhana such a one may be called the monk not empty of rizal is his jana he abides doing the master's bidding he is one who takes advice and he eats the country's arms food to some purpose what could i not say of one who makes much of the first jhana this in this suta the buddha is praising among who can attain jhana even for a short very short time as as short as a finger snap so here firstly we find that the buddha praises jhana praises the attainment of chana and this is quite different from uh nowadays some people not belittle jaana the other thing we can see from this is that jhana can be attained even for a short moment even a person they how long a person can attain jana and abide in ghana depends on his how long has cultivated it cultivated in a person is new has just attained jhana it's not very stable and he could attain it for a short time but with longer practice perhaps he could he can abide in china for longer and longer this jhana i just like to explain a bit this word jana is sometimes [Music] translated differently years ago they used to translate it as trans musing etc and more recently they translated it as absorption and mental absorption but the word jana literally means incandescence bright brightness so probably translation which is nearer the root word now would be a state of mental incandescence because a person who can attain jhana his mind becomes bright that's why when it's reborn into the jhana plane it becomes a being with a lot of brightness now we go to the next suta 1.21.47 the buddha said monks they partake not of the deathless who partake not of mindfulness of body and 1.21.48 they who partake of mindfulness of body do indeed partake of the deathless so here the buddha is saying that mindfulness of the body is extremely important it can bring us to the deathless state but here it uh it is my personal opinion that body here includes the mind also body sometimes because there's some other uh where the buddha mentions the body with the perceptions and mental workings so here even though it doesn't mention perception and mental workings i would assume that it includes the mental partner the mind because the body and the mind are the two compo two things that we associate with the cell the buddha said uh we always associate the cell with the five kandas and the five kandas if you analyze it now it is actually the body and full mental parts which constitute the mind
